sections.heading: Background
sections.body:
Semantic Kernel is an open-source SDK that allows you to
combine various AI services and plugins with your applications.
You can use Semantic Kernel for a variety of AI use cases,
including RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation).

By integrating MongoDB Vector Search with Semantic Kernel, you can use
MongoDB as a vector database and use MongoDB Vector Search to
implement RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) by retrieving semantically similar documents
from your data. To learn more about RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation),
see [Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with MongoDB](ai-key-concepts).

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To complete this tutorial, you must have the following:

- One of the following MongoDB cluster types:

  - An [Create a Cluster](create-new-cluster)
    running MongoDB version 6.0.11, 7.0.2, or later.
    Ensure that your IP address (Internet Protocol address)
    is included in your Atlas project's [IP Access List](access-list).

  - A local Atlas deployment created using the Atlas CLI.
    To learn more, see [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local).

  - A MongoDB Community or Enterprise cluster with
    [Search and Vector Search](manual/administration/install-community) installed.

- An OpenAI API Key. You must have an OpenAI account with credits
  available for API requests. To learn more about registering an OpenAI
  account, see the [OpenAI API website](https://openai.com/api/).

- A terminal and code editor to run your .NET application.

- [C#/.NET](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download) installed.

sections.heading: Set Up the Environment
sections.body:
You must first set up the environment for this tutorial. To set up your environment, complete the following steps.

Run the following commands in your terminal
to create a new directory named `sk-mongodb` and
initialize your application:

```shell
mkdir sk-mongodb
cd sk-mongodb
dotnet new console
```

In your terminal, run the following commands to install the packages for this tutorial.

```shell
dotnet add package Microsoft.SemanticKernel
dotnet add package Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.MongoDB --prerelease
dotnet add package Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.OpenAI
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions
dotnet add package SemanticKernelPooling.Connectors.OpenAI
```

In your terminal, run the following commands to add your MongoDB cluster's SRV (DNS Service Record) [connection string](manual/reference/connection-string) and OpenAI API Key to your environment.

```shell
export OPENAI_API_KEY="<Your OpenAI API Key>"
export MONGODB_URI="<Your MongoDB Atlas SRV Connection String>"
```

> **Note:** Replace `<connection-string>` with the connection string for your
> Atlas cluster or local Atlas deployment.
>
> Atlas Cluster
>
> Your connection string should use the following format:
>
> ```
> mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@<clusterName>.<hostname>.mongodb.net
> ```
>
> To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).
>
> Local or Self-Managed
>
> Your connection string should use the
> following format:
>
> ```
> mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
> ```
>
> To learn more, see [Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).

codeBlocks.content:
mkdir sk-mongodb
cd sk-mongodb
dotnet new console

dotnet add package Microsoft.SemanticKernel
dotnet add package Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.MongoDB --prerelease
dotnet add package Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Connectors.OpenAI
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions
dotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions
dotnet add package SemanticKernelPooling.Connectors.OpenAI

export OPENAI_API_KEY="<Your OpenAI API Key>"
export MONGODB_URI="<Your MongoDB Atlas SRV Connection String>"

sections.heading: Answer Questions on Your Data

codeBlocks.content:
Question: When did I start using MongoDB?
Retrieved Context: I started using MongoDB two years ago
Answer: Two years ago.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body:
MongoDB also provides the following developer resources:

- [Building AI Applications with Microsoft Semantic Kernel and MongoDB Vector Search](https://www.mongodb.com/developer/products/atlas/atlas-semantic-kernel/?tck=docs)

- [MongoDB Developer GitHub Repository](https://github.com//mongodb-developer)

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Semantic Kernel Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/semantic-kernel/)
>
> - [Semantic Kernel C# API Reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.semantickernel.connectors.mongodb)
>
> - [Semantic Kernel Using the MongoDB Vector Store Connectors](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/semantic-kernel/concepts/vector-store-connectors/out-of-the-box-connectors/mongodb-connector?pivots=programming-language-csharp)
